item.title: The Nancy Guthrie investigation could hang on a strand of DNA
item.description: A strand of DNA. An eerie doorbell video. The investigation into Nancy Guthrie's disappearance reaches 100th day with no sign of a breakthrough.

item.title: Trump heads to China today for high-stakes meeting with Xi
item.description: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

item.title: What to know about today's primaries in Nebraska and West Virginia
item.description: Voters are going to the polls in Nebraska and West Virginia on Tuesday, with Democrats vying for the chance to run in an open seat in Nebraska that the party has long been eyeing.

item.title: Mediator Pakistan allowed Iran to park military aircraft on its airfields
item.description: The effort potentially shielded Iranian aircraft from American airstrikes, according to U.S. officials with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: American climber dies in avalanche on Nepal's Mount Makalu
item.description: American climber Shelley Johannesen died in an avalanche on Mount Makalu, officials said Tuesday, as fatalities mount early in Nepal's busy spring climbing season.

item.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump says ceasefire is "on life support"
item.description: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran to make a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.

item.title: Gunman opens fire on Memorial Drive in Cambridge; 2 in critical condition
item.description: A gunman who opened fire at cars on Memorial Drive in Cambridge, Massachusetts, Monday afternoon was shot by a responding State Police trooper and a civilian.

item.title: Dua Lipa alleges Samsung uses copyrighted image of her to sell TVs
item.description: Pop singer accuses electronics manufacturer Samsung of using a copyrighted image of her face to sell TVs.

item.title: Trump pushes for federal gas tax pause as Iran war shows no end in sight
item.description: President Trump told CBS News exclusively on Monday that he wants to suspend the federal gas tax to give Americans some relief at the pump as the war with Iran continues. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has more.

item.title: Supreme Court clears path for Alabama to redraw congressional map
item.description: The Supreme Court set aside lower court decisions that had blocked the state from using a congressional map drawn by Republicans in 2023 that contained one majority-Black district.

item.title: Americans from hantavirus-hit ship arrive in U.S., including 1 who tested positive
item.description: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.

item.title: Why Americans from the hantavirus-stricken ship were taken to Omaha
item.description: Most of the Americans who were on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak were taken to specialized facilities at the University of Nebraska Medical Center.

item.title: Americans enter quarantine after returning from hantavirus cruise ship
item.description: The 18 Americans who were aboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship have returned to the U.S. and are now in quarantine. One remains in the biocontainment unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center after testing positive. Ian Lee has more.

item.title: Gymnast speaks out about alleged abuse by coach years after Larry Nassar scandal
item.description: Years after USA Gymnastics was rocked by the scandal of Larry Nassar, the disgraced doctor who pleaded guilty to molesting multiple young gymnasts, a gymnast alleges another coach abused her as a child because warnings went unheeded. CBS News chief investigative correspondent Jim Axelrod has the details.

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Alabama to adopt new House map
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday lifted a mandate in Alabama requiring the state to use a congressional map with two majority-Black districts. Wall Street Journal columnist Jason Riley joins to discuss his opposition to "racial gerrymandering."

item.title: Trump says he aims to suspend gas tax "for a period of time"
item.description: President Trump made the comments in a phone interview with CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes.

item.title: School principal who was shot tackling gunman: "It was just instinct"
item.description: Kirk Moore, an Oklahoma high school principal, took a bullet tackling a gunman in his school's lobby. He told CBS News what he did was "just instinct" and said he didn't even realize he'd been shot at first. Matt Gutman has more.

item.title: Texas sheriff says 7th body could be tied to shipping container deaths
item.description: The body of a seventh person was located Monday nearly 150 miles north of a Union Pacific rail yard in Laredo, where six bodies were discovered on Sunday afternoon.

item.title: 5/11: CBS Evening News
item.description: Americans enter quarantine after returning from hantavirus cruise ship; President Trump aims to suspend gas tax amid high prices due to war with Iran.

item.title: CDC leader says hantavirus not "a five-alarm fire bell"
item.description: Jay Bhattacharya, the acting director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, told CBS News that the hantavirus outbreak should be treated differently from COVID.

item.title: 5/11: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: President Trump says ceasefire with Iran "on life support"; Americans exposed to hantavirus return to quarantine in the U.S.

item.title: Supreme Court continues to temporarily maintain mail access to abortion pill
item.description: Justice Samuel Alito extended an administrative stay that maintained access to mifepristone through the mail.

item.title: White House ballroom funds hang over GOP push to fund ICE
item.description: The Senate is returning to Washington to resume work on funding immigration agencies with a package that includes $1 billion for the renovation of the White House East Wing.

item.title: Why hantavirus is not like COVID, according to infectious disease experts
item.description: Infectious disease experts have sought to reassure people that the hantavirus cruise ship outbreak poses very low risks to the wider public.

item.title: Years after sex abuse scandal, a young gymnast says "it happened again"
item.description: Sean Gardner, a gymnastics coach who trained elite young girls, will be in federal court in Mississippi on Monday facing 12 felony counts of sexual exploitation of children.

item.title: Number of police officers killed in line of duty decreased in 2025 compared to 2024
item.description: Though the number of police officers killed in the line of duty has dropped, non-fatal assaults against them have been rising since 2021, according to new data released Monday by the FBI.

item.title: Coast Guard seizes sailboat in Lynette Hooker's disappearance probe, sources say
item.description: The sailboat used by Brian and Lynette Hooker​ in their travels around the Bahamas — named "Soulmate" — has been seized by U.S. Coast Guard investigators.

item.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect Cole Allen pleads not guilty
item.description: Allen is charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump, assaulting a federal officer with a deadly weapon and two gun counts.

item.title: Trump says Iran's response to peace proposal "totally unacceptable"
item.description: President Trump didn't provide details on the issues he had with the response or what would come next.

item.title: 5/10: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship headed back to the United States. Trump says Iran proposal "totally unacceptable."
